architecture.summary: The system integrates a fine-tuned LLM into microservice deployment optimization by decomposing the NP-hard latency problem into natural-language sub-problems. A private Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) database supplies prior knowledge to the LLM. Chain of Thought (CoT) prompting with step-by-step inductive instructions enables the LLM to learn, reason, reflect, and regenerate code blocks for microservice deployment. Generated code blocks undergo integration and consistency assessment. Ablation compares RAG-augmented generation against LLM-only generation (without RAG) under identical simulation parameters.

biblio.title: Optimizing Microservice Deployment in Edge Computing with Large Language Models: Integrating Retrieval Augmented Generation and Chain of Thought Techniques

claims.statement: Our fine-tuned model significantly reduced latencies by 22.8% in handling surges in request flows
Our fine-tuned model significantly reduced latencies by 37.8% in managing complex microservice types
Our fine-tuned model significantly reduced latencies by 39.5% in processing increased network nodes
Our approach demonstrated marked improvements in latency performance over LLMs not utilizing RAG technology
Our approach demonstrated marked improvements in latency performance over reinforcement learning algorithms reported in other literature

The decomposition of the microservice deployment latency optimization problem into granular sub-problems enables the LLM to learn, reason, reflect, and regenerate code blocks with integration and consistency assessment
